Request a Demo
How It Works

From the MCC to a maintenance decision in five steps

Every SAM4 diagnostic starts in the motor control cabinet and ends as a validated maintenance alert. Between those points: edge processing, secure transmission, cloud AI, and expert review.

No sensors on the asset
No production shutdown
No IT integration required
Request a Demo

The SAM4 pipeline

From capture to recommended action, five stages. Fully automated. Fully managed.

1

Capture at the MCC

Current transformers and voltage taps install at the motor control cabinet. They capture current and voltage waveforms of every connected AC motor. Less than 60 minutes per machine. No production shutdown.

2

Edge Processing & Cellular Transmission

The Samotics gateway samples signals at up to 96 kHz, performs local signal conditioning, and transmits compressed data via built-in 4G/LTE cellular. No connection to your corporate network.

3

Cloud Analytics & AI

Electrical signatures are processed through AI models trained on thousands of industrial assets. The models detect anomalies, classify fault types, assess severity, and track degradation over time.

4

Expert Validation

Every AI-flagged anomaly is reviewed by a Samotics reliability engineer before an alert is sent. They confirm fault type, assess severity, write a recommended action, and estimate time to failure.

5

SAM4 Dashboard & Alerts

Validated diagnostics arrive in the SAM4 platform: asset health scores, fault alerts with severity and recommended actions, trend analysis, fleet-level analytics. Alerts push to CMMS via API.

Built to pass your IT/OT security review

Traditional monitoring requires corporate network access, on-premise servers, and ongoing IT support. SAM4 removes all three.

No corporate network

Data transmits via built-in 4G/LTE. No Wi-Fi, VPN, or firewall configuration.

Complete OT isolation

Gateway operates on separate network path. No access to PLCs or SCADA. One-way data flow.

Encrypted cloud

TLS 1.2+ in transit. AES-256 at rest. GDPR-compliant data residency.

CMMS integration

REST API. SAP PM, IBM Maximo. Alerts flow directly to maintenance systems.

Minimal-downtime deployment

How sensors install

Sensors install at the MCC. Motor back online same shift. Brief de-energisation only. Standard LOTO procedure. Less than 60 minutes per machine.

Each current transformer clamps around an incoming power lead. Voltage taps connect to the cabinet bus. No physical modifications to the motor itself—just cabinet-level hardware.

What you don't need

  • Sensors on the machine
  • Scaffolding or lift equipment
  • ATEX permits or hazardous area certification
  • Production shutdown or extended downtime
  • Corporate network changes
  • On-premise servers or infrastructure

Because SAM4 installs inside the motor control cabinet, we were able to deploy it across a large number of assets at minimal cost.

From your MCC to your dashboard in under 60 minutes

A 30-minute demo walks through the full pipeline—from signal capture to validated diagnostic—running on real industrial assets.

Request a Demo See the SAM4 Platform

Monitoring the assets your sensors can't reach.
